nodes.name: Birthstones & Anniversaries
nodes.description: Monthly birthstones and wedding anniversary gift traditions

items.prompt: Which deep red gemstone, named from the Latin for 'seed' due to its resemblance to pomegranate seeds, is the birthstone for the first month of the year?
items.explanation:
Birthstone for January; ranges from deep red to orange, green, and even color-changing varieties
Name derives from Latin granatus ('grain' or 'seed'), referencing its resemblance to pomegranate seeds
Mohs hardness 6.5–7.5, varying by species; almandine and pyrope are the hardest varieties
Bohemian pyrope garnets from the Czech Republic were hugely popular in Victorian-era jewelry
Tsavorite (green garnet from Kenya/Tanzania) and demantoid are the rarest and most valuable varieties
Smithsonian holds an antique hairpin set with Bohemian pyrope garnets in its National Gem Collection
1st wedding anniversary gift is paper; garnet is not a traditional anniversary stone but is given for the 2nd anniversary in some modern lists

items.prompt: Which purple variety of quartz, whose Greek name means 'not intoxicated,' is the birthstone for the second month of the year?
items.explanation:
Birthstone for February; purple variety of quartz (SiO2), colored by iron impurities and irradiation
Name from Greek amethystos ('not intoxicated') — ancients believed it prevented drunkenness
Mohs hardness of 7; durable for everyday wear but can fade with prolonged sun exposure
The finest grade is called 'Deep Siberian' — 75–80% purple with blue and red secondary hues
Once as valuable as diamonds until massive Brazilian deposits were found in the 19th century
Catherine the Great amassed a legendary collection of amethyst jewelry
The Duchess of Windsor wore a famous Cartier amethyst bib necklace to a 1953 Versailles gala

items.prompt: Which pale blue member of the beryl family, whose Latin name means 'water of the sea,' is the birthstone for the third month of the year?
items.explanation:
Birthstone for March; a blue to blue-green variety of the mineral beryl (same family as emerald)
Name from Latin aqua marina ('water of the sea'); ancient mariners carried it as a talisman for safe voyages
Mohs hardness of 7.5–8; tough and durable for everyday jewelry
The Dom Pedro aquamarine at the Smithsonian weighs 10,363 carats (~4.6 lbs) — largest faceted aquamarine in the world
Brazil gave Eleanor Roosevelt a 1,298-carat aquamarine in 1936, now at the FDR Presidential Library
Queen Elizabeth II owned a striking set of aquamarine jewelry including a tiara, necklace, and earrings
3rd wedding anniversary gift is leather (traditional); crystal or glass in modern lists

items.prompt: Which gemstone, the hardest natural substance on Earth and whose name comes from the Greek for 'invincible,' is the birthstone for the fourth month of the year?
items.explanation:
Birthstone for April; pure carbon crystallized under extreme heat and pressure deep in Earth's mantle
Name from Greek adamas ('invincible' or 'unbreakable'), reflecting its supreme hardness
Mohs hardness of 10 — the hardest natural substance; only scratchable by another diamond
The Hope Diamond (45.52 carats, deep blue) is the world's most famous gem, now at the Smithsonian; originally sold to Louis XIV in 1668 as the French Blue
India was the original source of diamonds, trading them as early as the 4th century BCE
The 4 Cs (cut, color, clarity, carat) grading system was created by the GIA in 1953
60th wedding anniversary is the diamond anniversary; 10th is tin/aluminum (traditional)

items.prompt: Which vivid green beryl variety, prized by Cleopatra and the Mughal emperors, is the birthstone for the fifth month of the year?
items.explanation:
Birthstone for May; green variety of beryl, colored by trace amounts of chromium and sometimes vanadium
Name traces through Old French esmeraude to Latin smaragdus to Greek smaragdos ('green gem')
Mohs hardness of 7.5–8, but most emeralds have inclusions (called jardin, French for 'garden'), making them brittle
Colombia produces the finest emeralds; the 858-carat Gachala Emerald was found there in 1967
The Chalk Emerald (37 carats) and Hooker Emerald (formerly owned by Ottoman Sultan Abdul Hamid II) are both at the Smithsonian
Cleopatra was famously obsessed with emeralds and claimed Egypt's mines as her own
5th wedding anniversary gift is wood (traditional); 55th is emerald

items.prompt: Which organic gemstone, formed inside mollusks and the only birthstone created by a living creature, represents the sixth month of the year?
items.explanation:
Birthstone for June (along with alexandrite); the only gemstone produced by a living organism
Name from French perle, ultimately from Latin perna ('leg of lamb') — describing the shape of the mollusk shell
Mohs hardness of only 2.5–3.0; one of the softest gems, requiring careful handling
La Peregrina ('The Pilgrim'), a 50.56-carat drop-shaped pearl found in Panama in the 1500s, is among the most famous; Richard Burton gave it to Elizabeth Taylor in 1969 and it sold at Christie's for $11.8 million in 2011
Kokichi Mikimoto patented the cultured pearl process in 1893, revolutionizing the pearl industry
Natural pearls form when an irritant enters a mollusk; the animal coats it in nacre (mother-of-pearl) layer by layer
30th wedding anniversary is the pearl anniversary

items.prompt: Which red variety of corundum, named from the Latin for 'red' and second only to diamond in hardness, is the birthstone for the seventh month of the year?
items.explanation:
Birthstone for July; the red variety of corundum (aluminum oxide) — all other colors of corundum are sapphires
Name from Latin ruber ('red'); colored by chromium, the same element that makes emeralds green
Mohs hardness of 9 — tied with sapphire as the second-hardest natural gemstone after diamond
The Sunrise Ruby (25.59 carats, Burmese) sold for $30 million at Sotheby's in 2015 — over $1 million per carat
Myanmar (Burma) has historically produced the finest 'pigeon blood' rubies from the Mogok Valley
In medieval Europe, rubies were believed to guarantee health, wealth, and success in love
40th wedding anniversary is the ruby anniversary; 15th is crystal (traditional)

items.prompt: Which yellow-green gemstone, found in volcanic basalt and even in meteorites, is the birthstone for the eighth month of the year?
items.explanation:
Birthstone for August; gem-quality variety of the mineral olivine, one of the few gemstones occurring in only one color (green)
Etymology disputed: possibly from Arabic faridat ('gem') or Greek peridona ('giving plenty')
Mohs hardness of 6.5–7; softer than most gemstones and sensitive to acids
Found in volcanic basalt, and also in pallasite meteorites — extraterrestrial peridot has been identified in samples
The Smithsonian holds a 310-carat peridot, one of the largest faceted specimens in the world
Cleopatra's famous 'emeralds' may actually have been peridots from the Egyptian island of Zabargad (St. John's Island)
The shrine of the Three Kings in Cologne Cathedral is decorated with 200 carats of peridot, long mistaken for emeralds

items.prompt: Which blue variety of corundum, whose name derives from the Greek for 'blue stone,' is the birthstone for the ninth month of the year?
items.explanation:
Birthstone for September; any color of corundum except red (which is ruby) — blue is the classic, but pink, yellow, and padparadscha (pink-orange) exist
Name from Greek sappheiros and Latin sapphirus ('blue stone'), possibly tracing to Sanskrit sanipriya ('precious to Saturn')
Mohs hardness of 9; tied with ruby as second-hardest natural mineral after diamond
The Star of India (563 carats) at the American Museum of Natural History is one of the world's largest star sapphires; stolen in a 1964 heist and later recovered
Princess Diana's 12-carat Ceylon sapphire engagement ring, now worn by Catherine, Princess of Wales, is the most famous sapphire in modern culture
Kashmir sapphires (discovered 1881) are the most prized for their velvety 'cornflower blue' color
5th wedding anniversary is wood (traditional); 45th is sapphire

items.prompt: Which iridescent gemstone displays a shifting play of color called 'fire' and is the birthstone for the tenth month of the year?
items.explanation:
Birthstone for October (along with tourmaline); a hydrated silica mineraloid, not a true crystal — contains 3–21% water
Name likely from Sanskrit upala ('precious stone') via Greek opallios ('to see a change in color')
Mohs hardness of 5.5–6.5; relatively fragile and can crack if it dries out (called 'crazing')
Black opal, the rarest variety, is mined almost exclusively at Lightning Ridge, Australia
The Olympic Australis, found in Coober Pedy, is the world's largest and most valuable gem opal
Napoleon gave Josephine 'The Burning of Troy,' a famous fire opal that has since been lost
The Andamooka Opal was presented to Queen Elizabeth II in 1954 during her Australian tour
10th wedding anniversary is tin/aluminum (traditional)

items.prompt: Which golden gemstone, scoring 8 on the Mohs scale and prized in its 'Imperial' orange-pink variety from Russia, is the birthstone for the eleventh month of the year?
items.explanation:
Birthstone for November (along with citrine); an aluminum silicate mineral found in pegmatites and high-temperature veins
Etymology debated: possibly from Greek Topazios (an island in the Red Sea) or Sanskrit tapas ('fire')
Mohs hardness of 8; hard but has perfect basal cleavage, meaning it can chip easily along one plane
Imperial topaz (pinkish-orange) is the most prized variety — named because Russian tsars reserved the finest Ural Mountain specimens exclusively for royalty
The American Golden Topaz at the Smithsonian weighs 22,892.5 carats — one of the largest faceted gems of any kind
Pure topaz is colorless; blue topaz sold commercially is almost always irradiated and heat-treated
Often confused with citrine (yellow quartz) historically; many 'topazes' in crown jewels are actually citrine

items.prompt: Which violet-blue gemstone, discovered in 1967 and found only in a small area near Mount Kilimanjaro, is the most recent addition to the modern birthstone list?
items.explanation:
Birthstone for December (along with turquoise and zircon); added by the American Gem Trade Association in 2002 — the first change to the birthstone list since 1912
A variety of the mineral zoisite; named by Tiffany & Co. after Tanzania, its only source on Earth
Mohs hardness of 6.5–7; wearable but significantly softer than the sapphire it sometimes substitutes for
Discovered in 1967 near Merelani in northern Tanzania; legend says Masai herders noticed blue stones after a lightning-caused bushfire heated surface zoisite
Naturally trichroic — appears blue, violet, or burgundy depending on the viewing angle and lighting
Tiffany & Co. launched tanzanite commercially, making it one of the most successful gem introductions in modern history
25th wedding anniversary is silver; the December stones have no specific anniversary association, but turquoise has been treasured for over 5,000 years
